title: xyris tomentosa
intro: Xyris tomentosa, also known as the woolly yellow-eyed grass, is a plant species in the family Xyridaceae that is native to the southeastern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Xyris tomentosa include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Xyris tomentosa is typically prepared for herbal use through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
